Alright, you're back. Thanks for digging into exercise. Starting to write out your vision statement and also your why. We're just scratching the surface here. Now we're going to start digging much deeper. And also, Victoria is going to start extracting ways out of me for people who have trouble doing this. For some people, it's very difficult to write a vision statement. Others, it's pretty easy. Victoria drags out these little secrets I have, shortcuts, acts, so to speak, of how any person, even if you're not good at this stuff, how you can write out an awesome vision statement that truly empowers you. In addition, I also start digging into the difference between vision and goals and start framing that for you. But I dig into that more deeply and other lessons to follow. So let's go right now to the interview. You're going to love this. So take us through the process that you go through to come up with your vision. Let's say someone has never done this before, never thought in that way. What's going on in your head? What are the steps that you go through to make that real in your head? Great question. And this is an excellent question. Number one, the first thing, let's start with things that typically end up stopping someone from creating a compelling vision is you have to imagine and just trust that you're capable of anything that you want. In other words, the enemy, you know what the enemy of great is in life? The enemy of great is good. Typically the problem with most people, and let's just digress for one second to goals, which is very different from visual talk about goals as well. But the problem with most people and their goals is not that they set goals that are too high and miss them. The problem usually is they set their goals too low and they hit them. And because of that, oh, I'm feeling okay. I hit my goal and they end up at this place of mediocrity. And when you're in that place where you're not really feeling pain or when there's no pain, there's no impetus to change. So what happens with people who are creating a vision, the same thing. If they create a vision that really isn't all that inspiring, because as they're creating the vision, say, well, I could never really own the big house. I don't really have what it takes to become a CEO or a sales manager or a top producer. They start doing their vision, such as writing out their vision, saddled by all the limiting beliefs from the past, not realizing that they have just been handed, essentially in this case, they're handed a gift. They've been handed a technique that can take them from being an average closer to being a world-class closer. That single distinction, that single change in their life, it all of a sudden opens up an entire universe of possibility for them. So what wasn't possible last month now becomes possible. So the trap is, is that when you're writing out the vision, number one, you have to think big. You have to go after and say, you know, on the highest level without being held down by my limitations that I'm only mediocre, which is not true. You, everyone, I don't care who you are, you're capable of greatness. It's just a matter of working to learn the skill sets that you need to learn, which is what you're doing now. But if you go into with that mindset that I am capable of anything, then you will write out a vision that truly is inspiring and that when you read it back to yourself, you're like, that is something that I, it's worth, you know, really going after because here's what happens. I will tell you this, bottom line. And I'll tell you too, there's an awful lot of hard work that stands between where you are right now and where your vision is. It ain't going to be easy. There's going to be work involved. So if you're not totally turned on and when you think about that vision, if it's not like, oh boy, you know, that's just, it seems like it's so worth it. Guess what? Your brain will say, eh, not worth all the work. So that's what happens. See some people, when they think about what the actual end result is, the prize, so to speak, at the end of the day, and the prize ain't so great in their brain, smart as it is will say, why do all the work? If the payoff is not that great, why do all the work? And conversely, if you actually are in a situation where your vision is so inspiring and the payoff is so dynamic and so fruitful and so empowering to that when you actually think about it and you read it, these are like, it's worth it. You will do the work. You'll go through the batteries, you'll go through the obstacles. This is going to be obstacles. It's going to be setbacks along the way. It's never straight line to success. We wish it was, but it's just not. It's going to be all these detours and problems and obstacles that come in that just kind of mess you up. Right? And if you have that amazing vision, though, that truly inspires, you'll say, it's worth it. I'll do the work. I'll overcome the obstacles. So that's the first thing that really you have to, you just have to drop the negative voices, the nay-saying voices of the past and write a compelling vision that is thinking big. That's about the best you can be on the high level. That's number one. The second thing that you have to do is you have to write it in the actual present tense. In five years, I see myself doing, not like in three years now, maybe I'll have this, maybe I'll have this. It's got to be, I am doing this. I am living here. I am driving this car. My wife is doing this. My children are attending these schools. My parents now are no longer struggling day to day. I am moving up now. I am working each day and feeling great. I see, and you start speaking in the present tense and your brain starts to make it real. So you want to project three to five years down the road, think big and bold, unlimited possibility. And by the way, of course, in five years, I want to be working in Jupiter and be the king of, I would like the US to give me the territory of Jupiter as mine. I'm going to be living on one of the Titan and I'll be ruling. That's not your vision. That's not reality. So it has to be, I'm not saying make some craziness up. I'm talking about keep it grounded in reality, but go to that highest level of reality. Because again, your brain also has a wonderful bullshit detector. If you create a vision and it's just simply all bullshit, your brain is like, eh, not real, not going to happen. And you won't do the work. You won't be able to break through the barriers and obstacles. So there is a balancing point. So I don't mean that. That's just delusional, right? Yeah, it comes delusion. Your brain is too smart for that. And what happens when you're lying in bed, your brain is like, we know, you know that I know. You know, you say it's really all crap, right? So it's got to essentially be grounded in reality, but at that high level, unlimited possibility. One of my, you know, uh, say my favorite businessmen who I really love and respect is James Cameron. He's a director of Titanic and Avatar. And I say he's a business man because besides being, you know, an unbelievable director and an artist, he's also a businessman. The reason why is I heard an interview that he did maybe a year or two ago when he, Avatar came out and they were like, you know, how did you stick with this avatar for 10 years and you dunk your own money into it? And you know, you just put everything on the line and you know, and you know, how is it that you have now two, the two biggest grossing movies of all time? What's the chance that one director would have $2 billion? It just seems outrageously impossible, right? He goes, well, I'll tell you what I do. And this is without even knowing anything about the intergame. Just naturally he revealed it. He goes, here's what I do. I think really, really, really, really big. This way, if I'm only half right, I'm still doing great. That's what James Cameron said. And herein lies one of the great secrets to a vision is that it has to be so amazing that even if only half it comes through, like, damn, I'm doing great. If your vision is only going to be great, if everything happens, typically it won't work out that way. And your vision is also malleable. It's constantly changing. It's updating. I always have a vision statement written out and I look at it often every day. And you know, that's not really true because here's what happens. In times when I'm just feeling so great and I'm living my vision, like right now, like I'm living my own vision, I don't have to look at it each day because it's just, it's me. I'm congruent. My life force is where is all my vision path. It's when I feel like I'm off path, that I make sure that every single day, sometimes twice, even three times a day, I'll put it on my screensaver on my phone. So every time I look at the phone, it's in my face, what I got to be doing, where I'm going in life to remind myself, to train myself not to get caught up in all the daily noise because that's the danger. The danger is that you have this unbelievably compelling vision, but along the way, there's the daily grind. And typically what happens is we get so caught up in our so-called problems that we end up focusing on the problem, living in the problem, so to speak, versus living and essentially going towards and focusing on the solution. And there's an old saying, more self development, where you focus on, you will go. It's almost a universal force of nature, karma, God, whatever it is. Where you focus on, you'll go. If you're focusing on something great, you'll move towards that. If you're focusing on what's wrong in your life, guess what? You'll crash right into it. So you need to learn to create that vision and then also focus on it every single day and listen, you have to deal with the obstacles, but you remain true. Your long-term course is on your vision. You know where you're going and you don't end up just focusing on the problem because you will. And I promise you, if you focus on your problems, you will crash right into them. You almost attract the things that you focus on. So just imagine, and this is another universal force that you attract to yourself, what you focus on, right? Not from 80,000 miles, but like, you know, when you end up essentially in your immediate area, you focus on something and move towards you. So what happens if you're focusing on all the crap in your life? You become a giant crap magnet. You actually attract crap into your life, right? If you focus on all the good things and all the things that you want, those things will move towards you. So again, all of that, which is like I gave you a lot of this key to self-development in one sentence there, but where does it all start? It starts with knowing where you want to go, having a target, having this compelling vision for your future. That's at the top, at the highest levels of the vision. Then beneath the vision, you have goals, which are short-term. Your goals come and go. You don't always hit your goals. Listen, how many times have you set a goal, worked your tail off to achieve it, and didn't? I have, right? So it's not like you're not so invested. If, oh my God, I didn't get my goal, oh my God, it's terrible. No, your goals come and go. It's your vision that's permanent. It's that vision that's where you're really focusing off in the long term. So you set a goal, which is sort of like almost like your stopping point along the way, and it lets you know whether you're moving towards your vision or away from your vision. But it's not the be all end, because here's what happens. If on the flip side, people have this way that they become goal-oriented, and that's great. I'd rather be vision-oriented. But if you're goal-oriented, the point where you say, I can't be happy, because I'm working towards my goal, and when I finally hit my goal, then I'll be happy. And guess what happens? You ever set a goal and achieve it? You ever have? Of course you have, right? What do you do when you get there? You say, great, what's next? So now you end up moving, so you're happy for a second, and you're like, what's next? So you end up being miserable all the time. You had a moment of happiness and a life kind of misery, because you're just setting goals and so invested in the outcomes. I set my goals, okay? And I then say, who do I have to align myself with to achieve my goals? What people will need to know, what organizations, what skills they need to learn? I put a date on my goals, okay? And I work towards my goals, but I don't become emotionally invested in my goals. My emotional investment is in my vision. My goals are the stopping points along the way, and I'm laser guided on my vision, but as I look at my vision out of the corner of my eyes, out of my peripheral vision, I'm always looking, analyzing opportunities along the way and saying, hmm, maybe I should look at that, maybe incorporate that into my vision. My vision is malleable. I said that right in the beginning. Your vision is constantly changing and evolving and growing, becoming better and more compelling as your own needs and your own, essentially your desires change as you grow yourself as a human being and that you learn your own lessons along the way. Your vision could very well change. My vision right now at 49 years old is not my vision. I assure you at 24 years old, back then my vision was just money raining down on the sky and I got it and realized it wasn't what was cracked up to be. Now my vision is more about contribution. It's about my family. It's about really living my life at a very high ethical level. The money comes automatically. When you're good at what you do, when you know the secrets to success, which is inner and outer game and being congruent and essentially having both of those worlds together. Understand that you have to essentially do both. You have to focus on the inner game and the outer game. The stuff that happens up here and the real world strategy is to get what you want. When you have that, you're going to make money. It's very easy to make money. But the idea is to live a happy, productive life while you're doing that and not to let it eat you alive, not to let it essentially get the best of you and let things like greed and greed supplant ambition, ambition, which is the healthy form of wanting good things for your life versus greed, which is about getting as much as you want as fast as you want and again, it all comes back to vision. It starts there creating that vision and then learning how to focus on that vision. Very simple. Wow. What can I say? Right? That was some pretty cool stuff. There's a lot I could say right now, but I want to just focus on one point here. There's something that I refer to as bifurcated focus. See, remember, when you're focusing on your vision with laser guided precision, you want to also make sure you don't get caught up in having tunnel vision, meaning that's my vision. So I have to focus on it. And no matter what I see along the way, if it's not true to my vision, I can't take advantage of it. Don't do that. We always want to be flexible, especially when it comes to vision. Your vision is a living, breathing, evolving organism. It's going to change as you move through life and your values change and also as opportunities come along. And what you want to do here is you want to focus on your vision with laser guided precision with one eye and then with the other eye, you're constantly scanning the horizon for other opportunities that come up along the way that you might integrate into your vision. You get it? One thing that any wealthy person will tell you is that they didn't make their money in exactly the way they thought they would. They had a vision. They were moving towards it, taking massive action, but it wasn't quite working at first, but then another opportunity that made sense to integrate and bam, just like that, they sort of success. So when we go through the next lessons here, make sure you always remember that when I talk about this, laser guided focus, it's always in the context of not having tunnel vision. That's a very important point. Now time for your quiz. Continue on with writing out your vision statement. I'm going to have more deeper templates. You'll see that allow you to just extract it out of yourself and please just take this really seriously. If your hands on your back, your neck are not standing up or you're not really just feeling pumped when you read this vision back to you, it's not compelling enough. It loses its power. Take the time, do it right, and I will see you in the next lesson.
